question: should Lumen Sketch focus on export motion or onboarding copy

councils.prompt: What does the graph say about execution pressure and constraints?

notes.title: Lumen Sketch
bullets: Lumen Sketch is a fictional creative tool about turning mood boards into small visual posters.
The proof moment is one export that feels worth saving without apologizing for it. Source: [[90 Sources/Captures/2026-05-21-demo-answer]].
The project should feel like a small creative ritual, not a productivity dashboard wearing perfume.

notes.title: Taste Profile
bullets: Mira likes warm interfaces with visible craft, specific motion, and emotional texture.
Mira rejects generic dashboard polish when it has no real point of view.
The Lumen Sketch export should feel tactile, emotional, useful, and not embarrassed by being pretty.

notes.title: Life Timeline
bullets: In April 2026, Mira started [[30 Work/Projects/Lumen Sketch]] after a week of messy visual experiments.
In 2026, Ari helped Mira see that the project needed one strong export instead of ten half-finished directions.
In 2026, River noticed Mira looked alive while tuning export motion and drained while writing fake onboarding copy.

notes.title: Pressure and Craft
bullets: Mira focuses better when pressure has a visible artifact attached to it.
Ten directions can become a hiding place when one finished artifact would expose the taste judgment.
The good pressure is not panic. It is the moment where the work has to become real enough to save, show, or use.
